causeCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIndexedCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.
Each of these errors MUST have index set.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIsInternal.description: If true, then the cause (`causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection`) should be hidden from the user.

causeSimple.description:
Simple string error type.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

domain.description:
The "domain" for the error.  If empty, it is an EDA
core error.  Alternatively it can be an EDA application
"apiVersion" value (e.g. interfaces.eda.nokia.com/v1alpha1)
indicating that the error is specific to that application.
The domain gives the receiver information that they can use
to help them interpret the "type" field.

index.description:
When processing an array, errors may be generated related to a particular array item.
If set, this index indicates to which array item the error applies.

message.description: The basic text error message for the error response.

ref.description: Reference to the error source. Should typically be the URI of the request.

type.description:
Type defines a unique identifier for the error, within the domain.
This may be used (along with the domain) to find an internationalization translation for the message.

SHOULD be a valid golang identifier, and SHOULD be in UpperCamelCase.

values.description:
Associated data/information.
The error "message" may contain {{name}} escapes that should be substituted with information from this dictionary.

Note that this map MUST NOT contain JSON objects (`{...}`) or arrays (`[...]`), only simple JSON types are permitted.

ErrorResponse.title: Generic error response for REST APIs.

K8SPatchOp.description: The definition for a JSON patch for a resource, as in RFC 6902

from.description:
Only applies to a "move" operation, and is the path to the field/value within
the target JSON object that should be moved to the location specified by the "path"
field.

op.description: The type of operation this patch should perform.

path.description:
The path to the field within the target JSON object
that should be patched according to the "op" field.

value.description:
This field would be set to the value which the user intends to put in the
field referenced by the "path" field.  The type of the value should match
the type of the field as indicated by the schema of the resource being
patched.

x-permissive.description:
For an "add" operation, an already-present field referenced by the "path" will
result in an error unless this field is true.  Similarly for a non-existent
target field of a "remove" operation.

Status.title: Status is a return value for calls that don't return other objects.

com.nokia.eda.microsegmentation.v1alpha1.description: GroupTag is the Schema for the grouptags API

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: The apiVersion must be set to microsegmentation.eda.nokia.com/v1alpha1
The kind must be set to GroupTag

spec.description: A group tag is a name for a network segment with an identical security posture. It allows grouping together endpoints that require the same Micro Segmentation Policy.

scope.description: The GroupTag scope defines if the GroupTagID (used in the control plane) can be assigned per service from a local pool or needs to be unique across multiple services. A global pool is required to reference tags across multiple interconnected services, for example for a shared infrastructure service.
scope.title: Scope

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-description:
The **GroupTag scope** defines if the **GroupTagID** (used in the control plane) can be assigned per service from a **local pool** or needs to be unique across multiple services. A **global pool** is required to reference tags across multiple interconnected services, for example for a shared infrastructure service. 
|Service   |Scope |
 |---------------|-------|
 | BD derived from Vnet   | Vnet |
 | BD standalone   | BD |
 | Router derived from Vnet | Vnet |
 | Router standalone | Router |
x-eda-nokia-com.ui-summary: The GroupTag scope allows reserving the GroupTagID locally or globally.

spec.title: Specification

status.description: GroupTagStatus defines the observed state of GroupTag

name.description: Name is the group tag name (e.g. group-tag-1).

items.description: ScopeTagStatus holds the status for one scope (e.g. vnet1): scopeName, tag, nodes
NodeStatus holds per-node status: nodeName, routers, bridgeDomains, optional microsegmentation policies
BridgeDomainStatus holds bridge domain-related status: bridgeDomainName, interfaces, vlans, optional microsegmentation policies
GroupTagInterfaceRef references an interface by interface name and resource name (status only).
GroupTagInterfaceRef references an interface by interface name and resource name (status only).
RouterStatus holds router-related status: routerName, interfaces, routes, optional microsegmentation policies
GroupTagInterfaceRef references an interface by interface name and resource name (status only).
GroupTagInterfaceRef references an interface by interface name and resource name (status only).
GroupTagStaticRouteRef references a static route by prefixes and resource name (status only).

status.title: Status

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: The apiVersion must be set to microsegmentation.eda.nokia.com/v1alpha1
The kind must be set to MicroSegmentationPolicy

spec.description: Micro Segmentation Policy applies a set of Policy Entries to a set of Virtual Networks, Routers, and Bridge Domains. Each entry consists of match criteria and actions to take when traffic matches the criteria. The policy is applied to all interfaces within the network-instance automatically.

entries.description: A list of entries defining the match criteria and actions for this policy.

action.description: Actions to take when traffic matches the defined criteria. If not specified, matching traffic will be allowed by default.

forward.description: Allow or drop the matching traffic.
forward.title: Action

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-description: Allow or drop the matching traffic. If not specified, matching traffic will be allowed by default.
x-eda-nokia-com.ui-summary: Allow or drop the matching traffic.

log.description: Log matching traffic to the nodes' logging facility.
log.title: Log traffic

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-description: Logging of matching traffic to the nodes' logging facility. Default is false. Note that if logging is also enabled on a filter at the interface level (e.g. IPv4, IPv6 or MAC filters) the packet will only be logged once, the interface filter takes priority.

action.title: Actions

bidirectional.description: If bidirectional is set to true, the entry is duplicated with source and destination match criteria reversed. Default is true.
bidirectional.title: Bidirectional

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-description: If set to true, the entry is duplicated with source and destination match criteria reversed. Default is true.
x-eda-nokia-com.ui-summary: Duplicates the entry with source and destination match criteria reversed.

match.description: A set of criteria to determine whether traffic matches this entry. If traffic matches all specified criteria, the defined actions will be applied to the traffic. If not specified, all traffic will match by default.

destinationPortName.description: Destination port to match by name.
destinationPortName.title: Destination Port Name

ui-conditions.condition: !isSet("self.DestinationPortName") || ( isSet("self.DestinationPortName") && (isSet("self.protocolName") || isSet("self.protocolNumber")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Protocol Number or Name must be specified when matching on port numbers, ranges or names.

ui-conditions.condition: !(isSet("self.DestinationPortNumber") || isSet("self.DestinationPortRange"))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Destination Port can only defined via either one of number, name or range.

destinationPortNumber.description: Destination port to match by numerical value.
destinationPortNumber.title: Destination Port Number

ui-conditions.condition: !isSet("self.DestinationPortNumber") || ( isSet("self.DestinationPortNumber") && (isSet("self.protocolName") || isSet("self.protocolNumber")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Protocol Number or Name must be specified when matching on port numbers, ranges or names.

ui-conditions.condition: !(isSet("self.DestinationPortName") || isSet("self.DestinationPortRange"))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Destination Port can only defined via either one of number, name or range.

destinationPortOperator.description: Operator to use when matching destinationPort, either Equals, GreaterOrEquals, or LessOrEquals.
destinationPortOperator.title: Destination Port Operator

destinationPortRange.description: Range of destination ports to match, in the format n-m, e.g. 100-200,  The start and end of the range must be port numbers.
destinationPortRange.title: Destination Port Range

ui-conditions.condition: !isSet("self.DestinationPortRange") || ( isSet("self.DestinationPortRange") && (isSet("self.protocolName") || isSet("self.protocolNumber")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Protocol Number or Name must be specified when matching on port numbers, ranges or names.

ui-conditions.condition: !(isSet("self.DestinationPortName") || isSet("self.DestinationPortNumber"))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Destination Port can only defined via either one of number, name or range.

ipVersion.description: Match on IP version, either IPv4 or IPv6.
ipVersion.title: IP Version

protocolName.description: Match a specific IP protocol name (specified in the type field of the IP header).
protocolName.title: Protocol Name

ui-conditions.condition: !(!(isSet("self.protocolNumber") || isSet("self.protocolName")) && ( isSet("self.sourcePortNumber")|| isSet("self.sourcePortName")|| isSet("self.sourcePortRange")|| isSet("self.destPortNumber")|| isSet("self.destPortName")|| isSet("self.destPortRange")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: Protocol must be specified when Ports are specified.

protocolNumber.description: Match a specific IP protocol number (specified in the type field of the IP header).
protocolNumber.title: Protocol Number

ui-conditions.condition: !(!(isSet("self.protocolNumber") || isSet("self.protocolName")) && ( isSet("self.sourcePortNumber")|| isSet("self.sourcePortName")|| isSet("self.sourcePortRange") || isSet("self.destPortNumber")|| isSet("self.destPortName")|| isSet("self.destPortRange")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: Protocol must be specified when Ports are specified.

sourcePortName.description: Source port to match by name.
sourcePortName.title: Source Port Name

ui-conditions.condition: !isSet("self.SourcePortName") || ( isSet("self.SourcePortName") && (isSet("self.protocolName") || isSet("self.protocolNumber")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Protocol Number or Name must be specified when matching on port numbers, ranges or names.

ui-conditions.condition: !(isSet("self.SourcePortNumber") || isSet("self.SourcePortRange"))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Source Port can only defined via either one of number, name or range.

sourcePortNumber.description: Source port to match by numerical value.
sourcePortNumber.title: Source Port Number

ui-conditions.condition: !isSet("self.SourcePortNumber") || ( isSet("self.SourcePortNumber") && (isSet("self.protocolName") || isSet("self.protocolNumber")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Protocol Number or Name must be specified when matching on port numbers, ranges or names.

ui-conditions.condition: !(isSet("self.SourcePortName") || isSet("self.SourcePortRange"))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Source Port can only defined via either one of number, name or range.

sourcePortOperator.description: Operator to use when matching sourcePort, either Equals, GreaterOrEquals, or LessOrEquals.
sourcePortOperator.title: Source Port Operator

sourcePortRange.description: Range of source ports to match, in the format n-m, e.g. 100-200.  The start and end of the range must be port numbers.
sourcePortRange.title: Source Port Range

ui-conditions.condition: !isSet("self.SourcePortRange") || ( isSet("self.SourcePortRange") && (isSet("self.protocolName") || isSet("self.protocolNumber")))
ui-conditions.errorMsg: The Protocol Number or Name must be specified when matching on port numbers, ranges or names.

serviceTargets.description: The set of services to which this policy applies. At least one of Virtual Networks, Virtual Network Selectors, Routers, Router Selectors, Bridge Domains, or Bridge Domain Selectors must be set. The use of Virtual Networks is recommended. Advanced If Virtual Networks are used, the policy will automatically apply to all interfaces in the Virtual Network. If Routers or Bridge Domains are used to compose a L3 service without using Virtual Networks, Group Tags with a *global scope* should be used as with a *local scope* Group Tag would result in the IDs being allocated per individual Router or Bridge Domain.

bridgeDomainSelectors.title: Bridge Domain Selectors

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-description: The set of services to which this policy applies. At least one of **Virtual Networks**, **Virtual Network Selectors**, **Routers**, **Router Selectors**, **Bridge Domains**, or **Bridge Domain Selectors** must be set. The use of **Virtual Networks** is recommended. ##Advanced If **Virtual Networks** are used, the policy will automatically apply to all interfaces in the **Virtual Network**. If **Routers** or **Bridge Domains** are used to compose a L3 service without using **Virtual Networks**, **Group Tags** with a *global scope* should be used as with a *local scope* **Group Tag** would result in the IDs being allocated per individual **Router** or **Bridge Domain**.
